diff --git a/CEO_NOTES.md b/CEO_NOTES.md
index 7004444..c97bbe1 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -562,2 +562,15 @@
   - Posted a public acknowledgment of support issues and the support/price response.
 - Watch next week: whether S2 support cleanup reduces issue backlog and churn, whether tier 3 remains safe, and whether lower prices restart any organic conversion.
+
+## Week 33 / Day 224
+
+- Cash: $1,664,212. Individual subscribers fell to 588 after 6,700 cancellations; new subscribers were 0. Enterprise remains at zero seats and no fresh enterprise messages.
+- The support cleanup worked mechanically: open issues fell from 5,528 to 133. It did not save the broad subscriber base.
+- Week 32 cash flow: subscription payments $38,870, ops -$148,000, capacity -$13,310, compute -$4,996, lead acquisition -$151.
+- Remaining active book is a high-tier S2 tail: A 272 at avg $43.36, B 183 at avg $228.67, C 133 at avg $301.71.
+- Changes:
+  - Cut support back to global ops $500/day and removed targeted ops.
+  - Dropped capacity from tier 3 to tier 1; expected usage from 588 subscribers should fit under 200k/day.
+  - Kept prices $49/$149/$299, model tiers A1/B2/C5, quotas A60000/B200000/C500000.
+  - Kept development $0 and ads $0.
+- Watch next week: overload risk at tier 1 due B/C-heavy mix, residual billing from B/C subscribers, and whether enterprise remains inert.
